what: Remove unnecessary .to(tl.int64) cast in FP8 V load offset computation — max offset fits int32 (256*8192*576+511 < 2^31). Int64 ops are 2x slower on CDNA.
why: Inner-loop int64 offset calculation is a compute bottleneck; int32 arithmetic halves the ALU cost for V loads

what: Use MXFP4 data for V accumulation: dequant first 512 dims from packed FP4 data using tl.dot_scaled with identity-like structure, avoiding separate FP8 V load (saves 512 bytes/token, replaces with ~272 bytes already loaded for K)
why: V load is the dominant bandwidth cost (512 FP8 bytes vs 306 MXFP4 bytes for K). Reusing MXFP4 data for V eliminates redundant memory traffic.

what: Reduce stage2 num_warps from 4 to 1 — stage2 only reads NSPLIT (8 or 16) values and combines them, wastes 3 warps of resources
why: Stage2 is trivially lightweight per workgroup (reads 8-16 floats + 8-16 bf16 vectors). 4 warps per workgroup wastes VGPR and occupancy for no benefit.
